text: Lesson 27
zh: 第27课

text: Nothing to sell and nothing to buy
zh: 一无所卖，一无所买

text: What is the most important thing for a tramp?
zh: 对流浪汉来说，什么才是最重要的？

text: It has been said that everyone lives by selling something.
zh: 有人说，每个人都是靠出卖某种东西为生的。

text: In the light of this statement, teachers live by selling knowledge,
zh: 按照这种说法，教师靠出卖知识为生，

text: philosophers by selling wisdom and priests by selling spiritual comfort.
zh: 哲学家靠出卖智慧为生，牧师则靠出卖精神慰藉为生。

text: Though it may be possible to measure the value of material goods in terms of money,
zh: 虽然物品的价值也许可以用金钱来衡量，

text: it is extremely difficult to estimate the true value of the services which people perform for us.
zh: 但要估算人们为我们提供的服务的真正价值却极为困难。

text: There are times when we would willingly give everything we possess to save our lives,
zh: 有时，为了保住性命，我们愿意付出自己拥有的一切，

text: yet we might grudge paying a surgeon a high fee for offering us precisely this service.
zh: 然而，当外科医生因提供这项服务而向我们索取高额费用时，我们却可能会心生不甘。

text: The conditions of society are such that skills have to be paid for in the same way that goods are paid for at a shop.
zh: 社会的现状是，技能必须像商店里的商品一样被付费购买。

text: Everyone has something to sell.
zh: 每个人都有可以出卖的东西。

text: Tramps seem to be the only exception to this general rule.
zh: 流浪汉似乎是这条普遍规律的唯一例外。

text: Beggars almost sell themselves as human beings to arouse the pity of passers-by.
zh: 乞丐几乎是把自己当作人来出卖，以博取路人的同情。

text: But real tramps are not beggars.
zh: 但真正的流浪汉并不是乞丐。

text: They have nothing to sell and require nothing from others.
zh: 他们没有什么可卖的，也不需要别人的任何东西。

text: In seeking independence, they do not sacrifice their human dignity.
zh: 在追求独立的过程中，他们并没有牺牲做人的尊严。

text: A tramp may ask you for money, but he will never ask you to feel sorry for him.
zh: 流浪汉也许会向你要钱，但他绝不会要你同情他。

text: He has deliberately chosen to lead the life he leads and is fully aware of the consequences.
zh: 他是自己刻意选择了这样的生活方式，并完全清楚这样做的后果。

text: He may never be sure where the next meal is coming from,
zh: 他也许永远无法确定下一顿饭从哪里来，

text: but he is free from the thousands of anxieties which afflict other people.
zh: 但他却摆脱了困扰其他人的种种烦恼。

text: His few material possessions make it possible for him to move from place to place with ease.
zh: 他为数不多的家当使他能够轻松地四处漂泊。

text: By having to sleep in the open, he gets far closer to the world of nature than most of us ever do.
zh: 由于不得不露宿野外，他比我们大多数人都更贴近大自然的世界。

text: He may hunt, beg, or steal occasionally to keep himself alive;
zh: 他可能靠打猎、乞讨，或偶尔偷点东西来维持生计；

text: he may even, in times of real need, do a little work; but he will never sacrifice his freedom.
zh: 在真正需要的时候，他甚至可能会做点零工；但他绝不会牺牲自己的自由。

text: We often speak of tramps with contempt and put them in the same class as beggars,
zh: 我们常常带着轻蔑的口吻谈论流浪汉，把他们和乞丐归为一类，

text: but how many of us can honestly say that we have not felt a little envious of their simple way of life and their freedom from care?
zh: 但我们当中有多少人能够坦诚地说，自己从未对他们简单的生活方式和无忧无虑的自由感到过一丝羡慕呢？
